query: double exposure photograph overlapping ghostly silhouette portrait
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / double_exposure: ghostly overlapping portraits, multi-exposure

response.objective_caption: A black and white composite portrait showing seven different views of the same woman arranged in a grid-like pattern against a dark background. Three faces shown in profile are positioned at the top left, top right, bottom left, and bottom right corners. Two three-quarter views appear at middle left and middle right edges. The central figure faces forward with eyes closed. The subject has short dark hair and wears a dark turtleneck collar visible in each frame. The lighting isolates each facial view against the surrounding darkness.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A black and white composite portrait with muted, faded tones and flat lighting that crushes shadows into near-total darkness. Seven different views of the same woman are arranged in a grid-like pattern, with the central forward-facing figure showing closed eyes and a slack, inward-turned expression suggesting sleep or dissociation. The remaining six faces in profile and three-quarter views display tense, anxious, or distressed expressions with furrowed brows and tight mouths. The flat, directionless lighting eliminates sculptural depth, rendering the faces as mask-like surfaces floating in oppressive darkness. The overall mood communicates psychological distress and fragmentation.
